question.text: The Black-Scholes option pricing model requires which of the following inputs?

answer.text: Spot price, strike price, time to expiry, risk-free rate, and volatility
Spot price, strike price, time to expiry, dividend yield, and trading volume
Strike price, futures price, open interest, volatility, and interest rate
Spot price, market sentiment, lot size, trading volume, and margin requirement

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** Black-Scholes 5 inputs: 1. S - Spot price of underlying 2. X - Strike price of option 3. T - Time to expiry 4. r - Risk-free interest rate 5. σ (sigma) - Volatility of underlying
**The Trap:** Trading volume, open interest, and lot size are NOT inputs. The model is theoretical.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Black-Scholes assumes: European options, constant volatility, no dividends (basic version), lognormal returns.
**Key Takeaway:** Black-Scholes inputs: S, X, T, r, σ. Five variables, one output (option price).
